metadata.title: Governance Primitives
metadata.context: Governance for ./project_spec.json defining project-local authority semantics, approval boundaries, admissible transformations, and review scaffolding.
metadata.purpose: Foundational Pillar of the project spec's invariant space. Used to describe what implementation must preserve or explicitly amend, not to restate agent workflow prompts.

invariant_constraints: The harness as a local CLI governance runtime.
Explicit separation between project invariants and current task authority.
Explicit separation between role contract, project context packet, output schema, returned report, and runtime probe.
PM-only, read-only behavior for the first vertical slice.
Structured JSON as the authoritative runtime artifact format for role contracts, project context packet, reports, runtime, and saved evidence where the source is object-shaped.
Deterministic orchestration for the first slice.
No agent file inspection outside the provided input.
No worker adapter invocation from the plan workflow.
No runtime capability claim without acceptance probe evidence.
No silent compatibility shims or legacy behavior preservation unless explicitly approved.
explicit_distinctions: Input/project context packet versus raw repo files.
Active implementation state versus archived history.

schema: Pydantic models used as output contracts.
Artifact shape expected by downstream runtime code.
deployment: Deployment is out of scope until explicitly approved in project spec.
Any packaging, install command, CI release, service, daemon, hosted endpoint, or background runner requires explicit approval.
destructive_operation: Cleaning generated outputs beyond explicitly safe temporary files.
broad_architecture: Replacing the deterministic orchestrator with an LLM manager.
Allowing agents to choose arbitrary next agents.
compatibility_and_fallback_commitment: Clean replacement is preferred unless compatibility is explicitly required.
project_intent_dependent_behavior: Requires explicit approval when behavior depends on a claim about what the project should become rather than what the current spec already authorizes.

order.description: Explicit amendments to the project spec or governance primitives approved by project authority.
This file and the other docs under ../project_spec/**
Open decisions that interpret the project spec without silently overriding it.
Active plans and trackers implementing an already-authorized objective.
Current user instructions selecting, sequencing, or pausing already-authorized work.
Archived implementation history and prior chat context.

task_authority.description: Current instructions and active plans have authority to select, sequence, and pause work towards already-authorized objectives, but they do not have authority to silently override project invariants or prior authority.
task_authority.conflict_handling: If task authority conflicts with invariant authority, stop and surface the conflict instead of improvising around it.
task_authority.task_authorization: The user and project manager will work together utilizing the project manager report to authorize tasks.
agent_authority_boundary: Agent will not redefine project intent.
Agent will not invent governance rules.
Agent will not invent acceptance criteria.
Agent will not authorize approval-boundary crossings by implication.
Agent will not treat missing context as present.
Agent will not expand a PM-only slice into a multi-agent or implementation workflow.
Agent will not turn an agent recommendation into permission to mutate files.

authoritative_artifacts: Active implementation plans and trackers inside their authorized scope.
Saved runtime artifacts as evidence of what happened.
sources.order: Derived or presentation artifacts does not override authority.

fixtures_samples_test_roles: Fixtures and samples must not be presented as live runtime evidence.
A fixture report must be labeled as fixture or sample.
A manually pasted API output must not be treated as proof unless linked to saved runtime artifacts.
Tests must not silently repurpose sample files in a way that invalidates prior acceptance probes.
compatibility_commitments: There are no compatibility promises by default.
No legacy file path, prompt format, scaffold layout, or artifact format is preserved unless explicitly approved.

must_not_skip: PM-only route check for the first slice.

preserve_visibility: Unknowns must be named rather than hidden in polished output.

named_admissibility_inputs.task_constraints: Name the current user task and the authorized scope of action. Do not treat the task as authority to amend project invariants unless the user explicitly frames it as an amendment.
constraint_conflicts: Desired behavior and current approved slice.
named_admissibility_inputs.admissibility_checks: Must end with either pass, fail, or blocked. If blocked, name the missing basis.
stop_conditions: Requested work crosses an approval boundary.
Requested work expands beyond the current slice.
Requested work requires unauthorized file mutation.
Requested work requires an agent to inspect files directly.
Requested work invokes a worker or non-PM agent before authorization.

preserve_project_spec: Passing JSON context to the PM route.
Improving error messages for missing required context.
Treating optional sources as missing rather than fatal when allowed by context policy.
Removing obsolete TOML/Markdown PM loading if replaced by the approved JSON-agent path.
Keeping the first route PM-only and read-only.
require_open_decision: Choosing JSON Schema versus Pydantic versus both for validation.
Choosing how run IDs are named beyond timestamp paths.
When to convert codebase that would benefit from Rust, and what to leave as Python.
amend_project_spec_or_governance: Changing the project from CLI-first to UI-first.
Allowing agents to inspect files directly.
Allowing agents to run shell commands.
Making Codex worker execution part of the plan workflow.
Replacing repo-local artifacts with external storage.
Making archived bundles current by default.
preserve_provenance_or_inspectability: Worker adapter execution when later introduced.
forbidden_shortcuts: Hardcoding PM report content in Python.
Duplicating PM semantics in runtime code.
Flattening structured context into Markdown as the authoritative packet.
Hiding failed probes behind successful summaries.
Silently continuing after missing required context.
Invoking non-PM agents in the first slice.
Giving the PM file or shell tools.
Preserving obsolete compatibility paths without approval.

source_authority: Whether each source was required or optional.
Whether the source was included, missing, or invalid.

visible_before_closing_work: Updated implementation plan/tracker if active work exists.
Known failures updated or ruled out.
Archive summary if a bundle closes.

acceptance_probes.question: Can the harness run the first PM planning slice from the CLI using the JSON agent contract and structured input while saving valid runtime evidence and stopping before implementation?

not_proof: Validation without real model route where the claim concerns model call behavior.
PM statement that the probe passed without deterministic probe evidence.
A run that invokes planner, adversary, reviewer, implementer, archivist, or worker execution.
